questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Plane wave fronts are incident are incident on a glass slab which has refractive index as a function of distance $Z$, according to the relation $\mu=\mu_0\left(1-\frac{Z^2}{Z_0^2}\right)$, where $\mu_0$ is the refractive index along the axis and $Z_0$ is a constant. This glass slab can act as lens of focal length $F(Z \ll F)$. (Incident rays are normal to the slab)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Position of object O and screen is fixed and lens is moved. At two positions of lens we get clear image on the screen. First position is at 30 cm from object and second position is at 50 cm from object. Find focal length of lens (in cm)
questions.solution: $V$ and $U$ will get interchange on changing object position. $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{30}+\frac{1}{50} \\ & f=\frac{1500}{80}=\frac{75}{4} \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1}}$ : When a concave mirror is held under water, its focal length increases.
$\mathbf{S}_2$ : When a convex lens is held under water, its focal length increases.
$\mathbf{S}_3$ : If electric field is zero at a point, the electric potential must also be zero at that point.
questions.solution:
$\mathbf{S}_1$ : The focal length of a concave mirror depends only on its radius of curvature.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{s}_2: \frac{1}{\mathrm{f}}=\left(\mathrm{n}_{\text {rel }}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_2}\right) \\
& \mathrm{n}_{\text {rel }}=\frac{\mathrm{n}_{\text {rel }}}{\mathrm{n}_{\text {surrounding }}} \\
& \mathrm{n}_{\text {surrounding }} \uparrow \mathrm{n}_{\text {rel }} \downarrow \mathrm{f} \uparrow
\end{aligned}$
$s_3:$ Since $E=\frac{-d v}{d r}$ : if $E=0$
$\mathrm{V}=$ constant not necessarily equal to zero
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A glass prism of refracting angle $\mathrm{A}=60^{\circ}$ gives minimum angle of deviation $\theta=30^{\circ}$ with the maximum error of $1^{\circ}$ when a beam of parallel light passed through the prism from air during an experiment. Find the permissible error in percent in the measurement of refractive index $\mu$ of the material of the prism
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
